title: 9 Herbal Tinctures For Swollen Gums
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a great way to soothe swollen gums. You see, swollen gums, also known as gingivitis, can be caused by bacteria, inflammation, and poor oral hygiene. Herbal teas, which are made by infusing herbs in hot water, can help to reduce swelling and ease pain. The herbs used in these teas have anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm down the inflammation in the gums.  For example, Echinacea purpurea is an herb that's known for its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. It's often used to boost the immune system, which can help to prevent illnesses that cause swollen gums. Eucalyptus globulus, on the other hand, is an herb that's commonly used to relieve pain and reduce inflammation. Its antibacterial properties can help to kill off the bacteria that cause gingivitis. Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, is another herb that's been used for centuries to soothe swollen gums. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and ease pain.  When you drink herbal teas made with these herbs, the active compounds are absorbed into your body, where they can help to reduce inflammation and ease pain. This can bring several benefits to your life. For one, it can help to reduce the discomfort and pain associated with swollen gums, making it easier to eat and speak. It can also help to prevent further complications, such as tooth loss or gum recession. Additionally, using herbal teas can be a natural and non-invasive way to manage swollen gums, without relying on harsh chemicals or antibiotics.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures contains alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and phenolic acids. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling. The alkylamides, particularly cis-9-kaur-16-enylisobutyramide, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, such as prostaglandins and leukotrienes. This can lead to a decrease in swelling and inflammation in the gums. The anti-inflammatory properties of Echinacea purpurea tinctures may be beneficial in addressing swollen gums caused by infection or inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Echinacea purpurea roots and flowers.
Combine the roots and flowers in a clean glass jar.
Add 2 cups of 80% vodka to the jar. The liquid should cover the Echinacea.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tinctures contains eucalyptol and cineole, two potent compounds that help reduce swelling in gums. Eucalyptol is a natural anti-inflammatory that inhibits the production of prostaglandins, which cause pain and swelling. Cineole has antiseptic and antibacterial properties, helping to prevent infections that can cause gum inflammation. By reducing inflammation and preventing infection, eucalyptus globulus tinctures helps to soothe swollen gums and promote healing. The antiseptic properties of cineole also help to eliminate bacteria that can cause gum disease.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal tinctures for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 2 cups of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Combine the Eucalyptus leaves and vodka in the jar. Make sure the leaves are completely covered by the vodka.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the tincture mix.
After 2 weeks, strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Label the jar and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops of the tincture in water, 3-4 times a day, to help soothe swollen gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis tinctures contains rosmarinic acid, a powerful antioxidant that helps reduce swelling in the gums. This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which directly address the underlying cause of swollen gums. The antimicrobial properties of rosmarinic acid also prevent the growth of bacteria that contribute to gum infections. Salvia officinalis tinctures also contain flavonoids, such as luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory effects that help soothe swollen gums. By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth, Salvia officinalis tinctures can help alleviate swollen gums.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal tinctures for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves and 2 cups of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Cover the leaves with vodka and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and let it mature for 2 more weeks before using. Take 10-20 drops, 3 times a day for swollen gums.
Label and date the jar and store it safely out of reach of children. Consult a healthcare professional before using the tincture if you have any health concerns.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures contains compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds work by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as COX-2, that contribute to swelling and pain in the gums. The tincture also contains gingerols' active metabolite, 6-gingerol, which has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory effects. The anti-inflammatory properties of Zingiber officinale tinctures help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums by decreasing inflammation and promoting healing. This makes it a useful remedy for swollen gums, particularly those caused by gum diseases or injuries.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal tinctures for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh ginger roots, 2 cups of 80-proof vodka, and a clean glass jar.
Chop the ginger roots into small pieces and put them in the glass jar.
Pour the vodka over the ginger roots, making sure they are completely covered.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar, discarding the ginger solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures contains compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling. These compounds also have antioxidant properties, which can protect gum tissue from damage caused by free radicals. Glycyrrhizin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of enzymes that break down gum tissue, allowing it to heal more quickly. Flavonoids, on the other hand, can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with swollen gums. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures can help alleviate swollen gums caused by gum disease or other conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tinctures for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 2 cups of 80% vodka in clean glass containers.
Cut the roots into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 80% vodka over the roots, making sure they are completely covered.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tinctures contains alkaloids like quinine and cinchonine, which are known for their anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and pain in the gums by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are chemical messengers that trigger inflammation. The tannins in Cinchona officinalis also contribute to its anti-inflammatory effects by reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, the alkaloids in the tincture help constrict blood vessels, which can help reduce blood flow to the affected area and minimize swelling. By using Cinchona officinalis tinctures, the swollen gums may experience relief from inflammation and pain.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal tinctures for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Cinchona officinalis bark, a 1-cup glass jar, a coffee filter, and a measuring cup.
Place the Cinchona officinalis bark in the glass jar and fill it with 1 cup of vodka.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through the coffee filter into another glass jar, discarding the solids.
Label the jar with the date and contents, and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 10-20 drops of the tincture in water to help with swollen gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tinctures contains the bioactive constituents menthone, limonene, and menthol. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce swelling and infection in swollen gums. Menthone and limonene work together to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria, while menthol helps to numb the area and ease pain. The antimicrobial properties of Mentha x piperita tinctures help to eliminate the underlying cause of swollen gums, such as gum disease or a bacterial infection. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, Mentha x piperita tinctures helps to promote healing and reduce discomfort in swollen gums.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal tinctures for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Mentha x piperita leaves and 2 cups of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Add the fresh leaves to the jar, leaving 1 inch of space at the top.
Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place. To use, take 10-20 drops of the tincture in water 3-4 times a day for swollen gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures contains potent anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin. These bioactive constituents help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe-emodin also help to reduce pain and discomfort in the affected area. Aloin has antioxidant properties that help to protect the gums from further damage and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, aloe barbadensis tinctures can help alleviate swollen and painful gums.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal tinctures for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel, 2 cups of 80% vodka, and a clean glass jar.
Cut the Aloe barbadensis into small pieces and place them in the glass jar.
Pour the vodka over the Aloe pieces, making sure they are completely covered.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids and use the tincture as needed for swollen gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tinctures contains thymol, a key bioactive constituent that helps reduce inflammation and swelling in gums. Thymol has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which fight off bacteria and calm down irritated tissues. This action helps to ease swelling and redness in gums, making it a good remedy for swollen gums. Thymol also has antiseptic properties, which help prevent infection and promote healing in the affected area. By reducing inflammation and fighting off bacteria, Thymus vulgaris tinctures helps to alleviate swollen gums and promote overall oral health.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal tinctures for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves and 2 cups of 80 proof vodka.
Combine the Thymus vulgaris leaves and vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the Thymus vulgaris tincture in the jar and take 10-20 drops in water as needed for swollen gums.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent swollen gums from forming. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the gums. They can also help reduce bacteria in the mouth, which can cause swelling. Regularly drinking these teas might keep your gums healthy and prevent swelling.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a soothing way to calm swollen gums, but it's essential to use them in moderation. Drinking them every day can lead to an imbalance of certain nutrients in the body. Limit your consumption to when you need it most, like during a flare-up, and switch to water when your gums feel better.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help reduce swollen gums, but results vary. Some people notice improvement within a few days to a week after drinking herbal teas regularly. For others, it might take a couple of weeks to see noticeable effects. It's essential to keep drinking the tea as directed to see the best results.
faq_time: For swollen gums, it's a good idea to drink herbal tea in the morning or early afternoon. This allows the ingredients in the tea, like calendula and chamomile, to work throughout the day. Drinking tea too close to bedtime might make it harder to sleep.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for swollen gums. Certain herbs like ginkgo biloba and St. John's Wort can affect how the body uses other medicines. This can lead to problems with controlling gum inflammation. It's best to be cautious when taking herbal teas with medications.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant and dealing with swollen gums, some women turn to herbal teas for relief. However, it's essential to be cautious with what you put in your body. Some herbal teas, like those with peppermint or chamomile, might be safe in moderation, but others, like those with sage or pennyroyal, can be too strong and potentially harm your baby. Always check the ingredients carefully.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing option for swollen gums, but it's essential to be cautious. Some herbal teas can pass into breast milk, so choose ones that are known to be safe for breastfeeding mothers. Peppermint and chamomile teas are often considered okay, but check the ingredients and potential interactions before consuming.
